Re: Foliow-up on Re(ruest for Formai Advice
re Wilson for Governor and Yes on 115
Dear Mr. Rothman:
This letter is to fo11o$/-up on my letter to you of llay 11,
l-990, concerning the dilernma in which the Yes on l-l-5 Comroittee
and the Pete Wilson for covernor Committee found thernsel-ves
because of the conflicting requirements of Proposition LO5 and
proposed Regulation l-8538.
We understand that you could not respond in writing before
May l-9, 1990, the closing date for the second pre-election
carnpaign report. 
As you know, this report governs all statewide
initiative 
disclosure required by Proposition 105 in connection
r^rith the primary electj-on. This letter is to set forth facts
that have occurred after May 11, l-990, and the decisj-ons taken by
both of these connittees.
Before }lay 19, 1990, the Pete wilson for covernor Conmittee
purchased a substantial television buy to support Yes on 115.
This was done with the knowLedge and coordination of the Yes on
l,L5 Corflmittee. The l{ilson for covernor Comrnittee sent a noti.ce
to the treasurer of Yes on l-l-5 informing him of the size of the
buy. consequently, the treasurer is now aware that the pete
Wilson for Governor Committee is the largest najor funding source
to Yes on LLs, as defined by covernment Code 584507(b).
Because you originally told ne that you believed that the
Proposition 105 disclainers would be required by the yes on l,l-5
Conmittee for a largest contributor who is a candidate controlled
cornrnittee (even though such disclosure confticts with the spirit
of Proposition 73 and currently vith proposed Regulation L8538),
and because you confirmed after receipt of ny May L1, 1990 letter
that you did not bel-ieve the staff vroul-d change its opinion, we
informed both the Yes on 115 comnittee and tha wiLson- cornmittee
that such disclosure is necessary on all yes on 115
advertisements .

we stiLl beli,eve that the Commission can determine for
either of the two reasons set forth in our May 11, 1990 letter
that such candidate comnittee disclosure is not required under
Proposition 105. However, this is now a subject for future
discussion, and we hope it will be looked at carefully after the
imnediacy of other pending questions that relate to the prinary
are answered.
We appreciated the fast response to our verbal inguiries and
your verbal confirnations after receipt of our Uay 11, L99O
letter. 
Early this Summer, our firm will try to think of pending
Proposition 1,05 questions and ask them early enough that there
wiLl be fewer last-rninute questions.

Re: Formal Rectuest for Advice
Dear llr. Rothman:
This letter is a fo1low-up to our conversation
regarding the application of Proposition l-05 (Govt. code
SS 84501:8451a)-to Pete wilson For Governor - 1990 and Crime
victims For Justice, Yes on 115. As you knolr, this firm is legal
counsel to both cornmittees.
Pete Wilson For Governor - l-990 is a candidate
controlled committee forned to support Senator Wilsonts campaign
for covernor. Crime Victims For Justice is a candidate
controlled conmittee formed to support Proposition 1L5 on the
June primary baI1ot. Senator wilson is one of the controllinq
candidates of this committee.
As we discussed, I an seeking formal advice regarding
the application of Proposition l-05 to the following situations:
Pete Wilson For Governor - 1990 witl produce and air a
television comrnercial at its o$/n expense (from noney collected in
amounts under Proposition 73 linits) 
in which Pete l{ilson
explains his support of Proposition 115 and urges voters to
support it. 
The Wilson Conmittee agrees that the cornrnercial also
fenetits Pete Witsonis candidacy. You confirmed that since Pete
wilson For Governor is paying all costs of the advertisement,
Proposition 105 does not require disclosure of crime Victims For
Justicers najor funding sources on the wilson ads.
-

ofi

a2


John Rothman, Esq.
May 11, l-990
Page 2
ouestion 1-: Does Proposition l-05 require Pete Wilson For
Governor to examine its canpaign reports to deternine if any
major funding source category applies?
Pete Wilson For Governor lril1 report the costs of the
spots on Schedule E of its Form 420 Report. Once Pete Wilson For
covernor airs the commercial described above, using the carnpaign
disclosure rul-es of the Political Reform Act of L974, as amended,
it has rnade either an independent expenditure to support
Proposition 115 or an in-kind contribution to crime victims For
Justice. But since Pete Wilson is a controlling candidate, we
assume the value of the cornmercial is reportable as an in-kind
contribution. 
But if this is true, does this non-monetary
contribution make Pete Wilson For covernor a rnajor funding source
of Crime Victims For Justice under section 84507 (b) ?
Senator Wilson would prefer not to be conpelled by
Proposition 105 to be disclosed as a major funding source of
Proposition l-l-5, which will pay for its advertisernents with money
that is permitted to be colLected in anounts not limited by
Proposition 73. As you know, at the Commission meeting in
october 1989, Senator Wilson publicly opposed the use of rrsoft
moneyfl not recej,ved within the limits by baIlot measure
cornmittees for promotion of a candidatets carnpaign for election.
Pete Wilson believes the Proposition 105 disclosure, if required,
would benefit his gubernatorial campaign in a rnanner inconsistent
with proposed regulation 18538 and the intent of Proposition 73.
In fact, a Crj,me Victirnsr ad that announces that Pete Wilson For
Governor provided major fundinq for the spot could be an i11egal.
non-nonetary contribution by Crime Victins to Pete Wilson For
covernor. We therefore request response to the following
additional guestions:
Ouestion 2: Is a candidaters controlled committee,
forned to support his or her candidacy, a rrpersonrr
meaning of covernment code section 84507 (b) ?
As we dj,scussed, it is doubtful that the
Proposition 1o5 intended to require disclosure of a
committee as a I'najor funding source.tr I{e ask you
question in the negative.
primari ly

proponent of
candidate I s
to answer this


John Rothman, Esq.
l{ay l.t-, 1990
Page 3
ouestion 3: Assurning a candidatets prinrarily-formed controlled
cornmittee is a rrpersonrr as defined in Government Code sections
84507(b), how is Proposition L05 to be reconciled with
Proposition 73?
It is Senator Wj,lsonrs position that the public poJ.icy
requiring expenditures benefitting a candidacy to be made from
funds collected in anounts unger Proposition 73 lirnits should
prevail over Proposition 105.' where the policies underlying the
two measures conflict in this manner, perhaps rrgenericrl
disclosure such as irrnajor funding provided by a (statewide)
candidate connittee" (or sonething sinilar) would serve to
effectuate both acts.
The prinary election is approaching very quickly. 
You
have infonnally tol,d me that Pete Wilson For Governor must review
its own carnpaign reports j.n response to question number one and
that Crime Victims must disclose Pete Wilson For Governor as a
najor funding source if the commercial is an in-kind contribution
received prior to the last pre-election report in response to
question number two. In view of the linited time before the
primary, please notify me imnediately if the information provided
in this letter has changed your position. 
we would appreciate
forrnal written advice on all three questions as soon as possible.
very tgdly yours,
rwE/bq

1 fhis conflict betlreen Proposition l-05 and 73 exists for
other candidates as wel1. For exanple, the press has reported
that the van de Kamp for covernor conmittee has made
contributions in excess of $1,00, OOO to the qualification efforts
of two and maybe three initiatives 
that he has chosen to rnake as
centerpieces of his candidacy for Governor, Iikely making Van de
Kamp for Governor a major funding source of these measures.
HILTACHK
